title: WordPress REST Revisions Management Flow

steps: Validate parent ID > 0; else return WP_Error(rest_post_invalid_parent, 404)
If post empty OR post_type != parent_post_type → return WP_Error(rest_post_invalid_parent, 404)
If post empty OR post_type != 'revision' → return WP_Error(rest_post_invalid_id, 404)
Call get_parent(request['parent']); on WP_Error return it
Check current_user_can('edit_post', parent->ID); if false → WP_Error(rest_cannot_read, 403)
Call get_parent(request['parent']); on WP_Error return it
Validate orderby='relevance' requires search param; else WP_Error(400)
Validate orderby='include' requires include param; else WP_Error(400)
[revisions enabled] Build WP_Query args (post_parent, post_type='revision', post_status='inherit', DESC order)
Map API params to WP_Query params (exclude, include, offset, order, orderby, page, per_page, search)
[out of bounds] Validate offset < total_revisions; else WP_Error(400)
[page overflow] Validate page <= max_pages; else WP_Error(400)
Build WP_REST_Response with X-WP-Total, X-WP-TotalPages headers
Add prev/next Link headers for pagination
Call get_parent(request['parent']); on WP_Error return it
Call get_revision(request['id']); on WP_Error return it
Call get_parent(request['parent']); on WP_Error return it
Check current_user_can('delete_post', parent->ID); if false → WP_Error(rest_cannot_delete, 403)
Call get_revision(request['id']); on WP_Error return it
Call get_items_permissions_check(request); on failure return it
Check current_user_can('delete_post', revision->ID); if false → WP_Error(rest_cannot_delete, 403)
Call get_revision(request['id']); on WP_Error return it
Check force param (boolean); if false → WP_Error(rest_trash_not_supported, 501) — revisions do not support trashing
Build WP_REST_Response with {deleted: true, previous: previous->get_data()}
Conditionally include: author, date, date_gmt, id, modified, modified_gmt, parent, slug, guid, title, content, excerpt
For date fields: call prepare_date_response(post_date_gmt, post_date) → ISO8601/RFC3339

flow_summary.get_items_flow: Request → validate parent → check permissions (edit_post) → check wp_revisions_enabled → query WP_Query → paginate → prepare each revision → return response with headers
flow_summary.get_item_flow: Request → validate parent → validate revision → prepare_item_for_response → return response
flow_summary.delete_flow: Request → validate revision → require force=true (no trash) → snapshot previous state → wp_delete_post(hard) → fire action → return {deleted, previous}
flow_summary.permission_delete_flow: validate parent → check delete_post on parent → validate revision → check edit_post (via get_items_permissions_check) → check delete_post on revision

key_decision_points: parent ID valid and matches expected post_type?
current_user_can('delete_post', parent->ID) and ('delete_post', revision->ID) — for delete
wp_revisions_enabled(parent) — whether to run WP_Query or return empty
force=true required for delete (no trash support)
key_relationships: get_items calls prepare_items_query, WP_Query, prepare_item_for_response, prepare_response_for_collection
Schema merges parent_controller->get_item_schema() for title/content/excerpt/guid fields
important_notes: Revisions do NOT support trashing — force=true is mandatory for DELETE (returns 501 otherwise). Routes registered dynamically per parent_post_type. Response fields are context-filtered (view/edit/embed). Dates returned as RFC3339 via mysql_to_rfc3339. Pagination uses X-WP-Total / X-WP-TotalPages headers plus Link: prev/next headers. Filter hooks: rest_revision_query (modify query args), rest_prepare_revision (modify response), rest_delete_revision (post-delete action).
